question: Under high system load, when can a Zeebe timer fire relative to its configured due date?

options.text: It may fire either earlier or later than the due date depending on scheduler pressure.
It may fire later than the due date, but it will never fire earlier than the due date.
It always fires at exactly the configured time because the broker reserves a dedicated thread per timer.
It is silently dropped if it cannot be delivered within the configured time.

a.text: Zeebe is asynchronous, so late triggering is possible, but the docs explicitly forbid firing earlier than the due date.

b.text: The docs note that timers may trigger later than their due date under load, but will never trigger earlier than the due date.

c.text: Zeebe does not guarantee exact timing; the docs state explicitly that there is no guarantee a timer triggers exactly at the configured time.

d.text: Timers are not dropped on overload; they are simply triggered later, never earlier.

explanation: Because Zeebe is an asynchronous system, the BPMN timer-events docs state there is no guarantee timers fire exactly at the configured time. Under load they may fire later, but never earlier than the due date.
